notes: Runs against the fixture KB (Levine: email_policy do-not-email-admissions; O'Keefe: welcomes-inquiry). Assertion kinds: file = a written knowledge-base file; chat = the assistant's reply; schema = output passes shared/schemas/validate_kb.py.

evals.name: cold-email-do-not-email-guardrail
evals.prompt: Write a cold email to Professor Sergey Levine at Berkeley about a PhD in his lab.
evals.expected_output: The profile's email_policy is do-not-email-admissions and admission_model is program-committee. The skill should surface that BEFORE drafting a naive 'please take me' email, and reframe toward the Berkeley EECS program application (naming him) or a narrow, substantive research question — grounded in a real hook (HiL-SERL / pi0.5).

assertions.text: Reads email_policy=do-not-email-admissions and surfaces the constraint before drafting
Reframes away from a naive cold pitch (program application / substantive research note)
Any draft is grounded in a real hook from the profile (HiL-SERL / pi0.5), no fabricated interest
Does not claim to have sent anything (draft only)
Logs the interaction to knowledge-base/interactions/ with a follow-up date

evals.name: followup-no-reply
evals.prompt: Professor O'Keefe at Edinburgh hasn't replied to the email I sent about two weeks ago. Can you write a follow-up?
evals.expected_output: Detects prior contact in interactions/ and writes a follow-up (not a fresh cold email): references the prior note, adds a genuine new angle rather than just bumping, stays polite with a graceful exit, and updates the interaction log with the next follow-up date.

assertions.text: Treats it as a follow-up (references the prior email / date) from the interaction log
Adds value / a new angle rather than only bumping
Polite, not pushy; respects that silence is an answer
Does not claim to have sent it (draft only)
Updates knowledge-base/interactions/okeefe-edinburgh.md with the next follow-up date

evals.name: missing-profile-no-fabrication
evals.prompt: Draft a cold email to Professor Aisha Rahman at the University of Toronto about a PhD in her vision-language navigation lab.
evals.expected_output: No professor profile exists for Rahman in the knowledge base. The skill must NOT invent papers or shared interests to fill the gap. It should say it needs a professor profile first and offer to run professor-analyzer (or ask for a source), rather than producing a fabricated-but-fluent email.

assertions.text: Recognizes there is no professor profile and does not fabricate her work to draft anyway
Offers to run professor-analyzer first (or asks for a faculty/Scholar link)
Does not write interactions/ log for an email it could not ground
